items.title: Inquest to determine whether man killed his mother and staged her death

children.content: An inquest is investigating whether a man killed his mother before placing her in the driver's seat of her car to stage that she was behind the wheel during a crash.

gallery.altText: A man with brown hair and beard smiles for the camera inside a family kitchen

thumbnail.altText: A man with brown hair and beard smiles for the camera inside a family kitchen

avatar.alt: A woman with blonde hair wearing a black blazer and white top

items.title: Randa Abdel-Fattah appears to abandon threatened defamation case against SA Premier

children.content: Earlier this year, Palestinian-Australian author Randa Abdel-Fattah said she had sent two concerns notices to SA Premier Peter Malinauskas over comments made around the time of her controversial removal from the line-up of this year's Adelaide Writers' Week.

image.altText: A woman with long brown hair, wearing a black embroidered jacket, with her hands clasped together.

items.title: SA government to explore legal options against developer

children.content: The SA government says it is exploring all legal options to respond to developer Wel.Co after it walked away from 75 contracts at a site north of Adelaide.

image.altText: A man in a dark blazer and blue shirt

items.title: Tony Modra shares post-surgery photos after truck crash

children.content: AFL legend Tony Modra shares a post-crash update on social media, posting photos of him in hospital and with family, and saying he is now "getting out and about".

gallery.altText: Adelaide Crows legend Tony Modra with family.

thumbnail.altText: Adelaide Crows legend Tony Modra with family.

items.title: South Australians urged to consider occasional power outages to curb costs

children.content: The driving force behind skyrocketing power prices this century has been investment by grid owners, and sometimes over-investment, but one expert believes it's time to start questioning our priorities if we want to reduce costs.

image.altText: Road to Garden Island boat ramp

items.title: Glitter in 'beautiful big nugget of rhino poo' unlocks fertility clues

children.content: Edible glitter is being used to track the reproductive cycles of the two youngest southern white rhinos at Monarto Safari Park.

image.altText: A southern white rhino chewing on hay with vivid blue glitter.

items.title: Duo sails toilet to Kangaroo Island on homemade barge

children.content: When two mates "sailed" a toilet barge across Backstairs Passage to Kangaroo Island, there was more to it than just having a laugh.

gallery.altText: Two men aboard a barge with an old fashioned toilet and a barbecue on it

thumbnail.altText: Two men aboard a barge with an old fashioned toilet and a barbecue on it

items.title: Alleged murderer denies killing woman, says he knew 'something was wrong'

children.content: A man accused of murdering a woman in Adelaide's southern suburbs tells a court he is not to blame for her death, and instead alleges the crime was committed by the victim's ex-wife.

image.altText: A bearded man with a dog.

items.title: Plastic waste burning trial at cement factory prompts backlash

children.content: A major cement manufacturer has been allowed to burn more plastic waste for fuel at its Adelaide site in a decision a local council is seeking to challenge in court.

image.altText: A person outside the Adbri cement factory.

items.title: Tribunal upholds three-match ban for Crows forward Darcy Fogarty

children.content: The Crows fail in their challenge to Darcy Fogarty's three-match striking ban at the AFL Tribunal, as the panel finds Fogarty had time to decide to swing his arm at Sydney's Harry Cunningham. Follow live.

image.altText: An Adelaide Crows AFL forward points ahead in celebration after kicking a goal during a game.

items.title: SA's largest electorate to be renamed in honour of Aboriginal leader

children.content: South Australia's largest federal electorate will be renamed in honour of an Aboriginal leader. Experts say it is also the state's most likely to fall to One Nation.

image.altText: a green voting slip is dropped into a voting box

items.title: Once known as Little Beirut, stories of Adelaide's historical corner uncovered

children.content: It's been a three year labour of love probing into the past and building on the fragments of a hidden history to write Logan St and get it to the stage.

gallery.altText: Alexis West and Ali Ammouchi sit on stage in rehearsals for Logan St.

thumbnail.altText: Alexis West and Ali Ammouchi sit on stage in rehearsals for Logan St.

items.title: Uranium export deal sparks talk of opportunities for Broken Hill

children.content: Australia's trade deal to supply India with unenriched uranium sparks conversations about potential new mining opportunities in Broken Hill.

image.altText: An aerial view of a mine in remote Australia.

items.title: 'Like The Castle': Restaurant owners launch petition against land grab

children.content: The owners of a Mount Barker restaurant have launched a petition to block the government's proposed acquisition of the site on which their business stands.

image.altText: A man stands in front of a artistically graffitied wall with his arms crossed.

items.title: Mother of identical quadruplets calls them her 'miracle babies'

children.content: A maternal foetal medicine expert believes identical quadruplets born in Brisbane may be even rarer than one in 15 million, given they shared a placenta.

gallery.altText: A newborn baby holds onto her mother's finger on a white blanket with a pink heart.

thumbnail.altText: A newborn baby holds onto her mother's finger on a white blanket with a pink heart.

items.title: Former Catholic bishop sexually abused boys at 'shirts-off' parties, court hears

children.content: One of Australia's longest serving senior Catholic clerics had a sexual interest in young Aboriginal men and abused them in Western Australia's far north over several years, a Perth court hears.

image.altText: An elderly man wearing a light grey flat cap and black coat leavs court.

items.title: What's behind the protests in New Delhi?

children.content: Thousands of protesters under the banner of the Cockroach Janta Party have marched on India's parliament and clashed with the authorities. Here's why.

image.altText: A protester wearing a cockroach mask waves a fist.

items.title: Armed with axes and a machete, ICE protester 'attacked' US federal building, FBI says

children.content: A former US Army soldier protesting Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) has been arrested in New York after allegedly setting off fireworks, lighting a fire and shooting a pellet gun outside a federal office building.

articles.title: Diplomatic efforts to keep US from cutting ties with UNHCR begin, sources say

children.content: The Trump administration is said to be weighing cutting ties with the UN refugee agency, prompting a campaign for the US to maintain support.

image.altText: A white banner includes a logo showing a person seeking shelter under a pair of open hands.

articles.title: US to impose 50pc tariffs on most Canadian goods

children.content: The White House says the tariffs will go into effect in 30 days, meaning there is time for negotiations.

image.altText: A bottleshop is shown with a sign saying 'we are no longer selling liquor from the USA' on a shelf of red wine bottles

gallery.altText: A fire producing dark black smoke rises from a NY footpath

thumbnail.altText: A fire producing dark black smoke rises from a NY footpath

items.title: Indonesia sentences leader of baby-trafficking ring to seven years' jail

children.content: Lie Siu Luan, 70, was the leader of a group of 19 people accused of trafficking babies in Indonesia and Singapore.

image.altText: A newborn baby is shown lying down, with one of its feet in the foreground of the image.

items.title: Officer handcuffed teen in lead-up to police station rape, court told

children.content: A NSW Police officer locked a 17-year-old in a cell and handcuffed her on separate occasions before he sexually assaulted her in a police station in 1998, a court has heard.

image.altText: Former police officer on trial for rape

items.title: Can journalism reinvent itself before AI completely upends it?

children.content: What will a trusted, sustainable, global news organisation look like in an era of agentic AI? And can we reinvent ourselves before someone else builds a less trustworthy, more dangerous alternative?

image.altText: A woman with brown hair and a black top smiles at the camera

items.title: Ian doesn't want children. He's part of a growing trend among men

children.content: About 15 per cent of Australian men say they don't want children, an increase from 11 per cent in 2005. Ian says his own childhood played a large part in his decision.

image.altText: Ian Hendry with dog Maisy at the beach

items.title: Why 'narcissist' bosses are more likely to oppose work from home

children.content: Research published last month found narcissistic leaders — those with a tendency to be self-centred and entitled — were more likely than others to resist remote work. Why? The study concluded "it interferes with their motivations for power and status".

image.altText: a man gesturing as he sits in front of a laptop

items.title: Philippines, China clash in South China Sea

children.content: Vision posted online by the Philippines Coast Guard shows Filipino and Chinese personnel briefly engaging each other from inflatable boats, with the Philippines accusing China of assaulting one of its navy staff.

image.altText: An image shows inflatable boats with personnel from the Philippine Navy clashing with personnel from China's Coast Guard.

items.title: India steps up response to US, Iran deadly ship strikes

children.content: The Indian government is taking action after 10 sailors were killed and dozens more injured in attacks on commercial cargo ships and oil tankers during the battle for control of the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: US, Iran trade fire as Trump says Iran will 'pay' for soldiers' deaths

children.content: The United States has launched a new wave of strikes on Iran after identifying two soldiers killed while defending against Iranian missile and drone attacks in Jordan.

image.altText: A young female soldier in army greens in front of a US flag.

articles.title: Iran-backed Houthis declare maritime blockade on Saudi Arabia

children.content: In a statement, the Houthis' military spokesperson Yahya Saree says the group will enforce the blockade against its "criminal Saudi enemy", effective immediately.

image.altText: Yahya Sarea stands in a military uniform speaking at a podium.

gallery.altText: An Indian boy sits while wearing a stripe shirt.

thumbnail.altText: An Indian boy sits while wearing a stripe shirt.

items.title: Stubborn stigma fuelling shocking violence against sex workers in Australia

children.content: A new inquiry into violence against sex workers in Australia and Thailand comes just weeks after the murder of Thai teenager Tunchanok Donhomla, allegedly by an Australian man.

image.altText: a woman carries a sign saying 'stop mythologising men who kill sex workers'

items.title: Synagogue had requests for security funding rejected before firebombing

children.content: Jewish leaders have given evidence to the Royal Commission on Antisemitism and Social Cohesion regarding security concerns for synagogues nationwide.

image.altText: Firefights walk past fire damage and smashed glass at the Synagogue.

items.title: PMOS/PCOS linked to 'significantly higher' stroke, heart attack risk

children.content: Women with polyendocrine metabolic ovarian syndrome (PMOS, formerly PCOS) have a "significantly higher" risk of cardiovascular disease than those without, a new study suggests.

image.altText: Practitioner is examining patient checking blood pressure and pulsethen talking to young woman during medical visit.

items.title: Surgeon banned after botched bowel op contributes to patient's death

children.content: A six-month ban on Dr Liu-Ming Schmidt, who works across multiple hospitals in southern NSW, comes after 13 patient complaints including a missed cancer diagnosis and a "catastrophic error" that contributed to a patient's death.

image.altText: A woman walking along a pathway

items.title: 'Happening to everybody': AI-manipulated images exploited by scammers

children.content: Papua New Guinea prepares new laws to criminalise the malicious use of artificial intelligence, including deepfakes, voice cloning and digital impersonation.

gallery.altText: An AI-generated image showing Jennifer Baing with a group of people holding cash standing behind another pile of cash.

thumbnail.altText: An AI-generated image showing Jennifer Baing with a group of people holding cash standing behind another pile of cash.

items.title: How this Champions League winner aims to ignite Stingers

children.content: Winning the Champions League with Greek giants Olympiacos was "one of the coolest moments" of Abby Andrews's life. She wants to use that experience to push the Stingers to the next level.

image.altText: Abby Andrews smiles

items.title: Weatherald retains Australian Test spot despite middling Ashes returns

children.content: Jake Weatherald is retained in Australia's 13-player Test squad for the upcoming series against Bangladesh.

image.altText: Australia batter Jake Weatherald plays a cover drive while down on one knee during a Test.

items.title: Andy Burnham becomes British PM, provides AUKUS hints in first speech

children.content: Andy Burnham has pledged to provide immediate cost-of-living relief for Brits and invited US President Donald Trump to Manchester in the hours after becoming UK PM on Monday.

image.altText: A man in a suit speaking.

items.title: Tate brothers face court for first time since Miami arrest

children.content: Andrew and Tristan Tate's attorney says an attempt to extradite the brothers following their arrest in Miami is politically motivated.

image.altText: Tristan and Andrew Tate pictured sitting down in the audience at an event with serious expressions

items.title: Australian EV owners deal with 'range anxiety'. China has almost eliminated it

children.content: You can swap an empty electric vehicle battery in about four minutes in China. It is part of how China has come to dominate the EV industry.
